title: oxytropis campestris subsp. roaldii
intro: Oxytropis campestris subsp. roaldii, also known as the alpine locoweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the legume family. The medicinal properties of this plant are derived from its roots, which contain a number of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides, that have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Oxytropis campestris subsp. roaldii include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antitumor properties, making it a promising candidate for the development of new medicines. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of dried roots, which can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
